stories.title: James Wood's Inside-the-Park Grand Slam — First Since 2022 Sparks Nationals Rally (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
James Wood just hit the first inside-the-park grand slam in MLB since 2022.

The ball caromed off an outfielder's glove, rolled to center, and Wood never stopped running.

Nationals were down 5-0. They won 9-6. #Nationals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Inside-the-park grand slams are so rare that the last one in MLB happened in 2022.

James Wood just hit one. His first career grand slam. Nationals rallied from a 5-run deficit and won 9-6 over the Mets.

22 years old. That's a player. #Nationals

facebook.long_form:
⚾ THE FIRST INSIDE-THE-PARK GRAND SLAM SINCE 2022 ⚾

Inside-the-park grand slams are so rare that when one happens, you stop and rewind the tape three times just to make sure you saw it correctly.

James Wood gave baseball fans that moment Tuesday night in Washington.

The 22-year-old Nationals outfielder sent a ball to left-center field that caromed off Morabito's glove and rolled toward center. By the time the Mets fielders tracked it down and got the ball home, Wood was sliding across the plate — not with a single, not with a double, not with a triple.

With a grand slam.

It was the first inside-the-park grand slam in Major League Baseball since 2022. Wood's first career grand slam. And it arrived when it mattered most: the Nationals were trailing the Mets 5-0.

Washington then did what baseball teams do when something rare and remarkable happens on their side of the diamond: they kept going. The Nationals rallied to win 9-6. Wood went 3-for-4 on the night, with Jose Tena adding another home run to the Washington offensive explosion.

Inside-the-park grand slams are the baseball equivalent of lightning: incredibly rare, impossible to predict, and once you see one, you never forget exactly where you were.

[Tag someone who would have gone absolutely wild watching this happen!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: James Wood hit the first inside-the-park grand slam in MLB since 2022 on Tuesday night — and the Nationals rallied from 5-0 down to beat the Mets 9-6. Wood's ball caromed off an outfielder's glove and he never stopped running. That's what baseball magic looks like. ⚾ #Nationals

article.headline: James Wood Hits First Inside-the-Park Grand Slam Since 2022, Powers Nationals' Comeback Win

stories.title: Schwarber Still Chasing History — Leads MLB at 20+ HRs, Bonds' Pace in Crosshairs (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Barry Bonds hit his 20th homer in his record 73-HR season on May 19, 2001.

Kyle Schwarber hit his 20th homer on May 15, 2026.

Four days faster than Bonds. If the pace holds, a 70-HR season is possible — which only two players in baseball history have ever done.

#Phillies

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber's 20th HR on May 15 was the earliest 20-HR mark in MLB history.

He's just the 8th player ever to hit 20 HRs in his team's first 45 games.

Judge is 2nd with 16. #Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
🔴 FOUR DAYS FASTER THAN BARRY BONDS. 🔴

Let's put Kyle Schwarber's home run pace into its proper historical context.

Barry Bonds hit 73 home runs in 2001. That is the all-time record. It has never been surpassed in the history of Major League Baseball. In that record-setting season, Bonds hit his 20th home run on May 19th.

Kyle Schwarber hit his 20th home run of the 2026 season on May 15th.

Four days faster than Bonds in his 73-home-run year.

Schwarber hit the 20-HR mark on the earliest date in MLB history — no player has ever reached 20 home runs in a season as early as he did this year. He is just the 8th player in baseball history to hit 20 home runs in his team's first 45 games.

The math for a 70-homer season is now genuinely in play. Only two players in baseball history — Bonds and Mark McGwire — have ever hit 70 or more. Schwarber is currently on a pace that flirts with that territory.

The Philadelphia Phillies are 21-23 as a team. Their entire offensive story right now is a 33-year-old left-handed slugger who decided this season was the one where he hits the ball into orbit on a historic schedule.

Aaron Judge is second in the MLB home run race with 16. Schwarber is four home runs ahead of him.

[Who else is watching every Schwarber at-bat right now?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Barry Bonds hit his 20th HR on May 19 in his record 73-HR season. Kyle Schwarber hit his 20th on May 15 — four days faster. He's on pace for 67-70 homers, which would be only the second time in baseball history anyone has done that. The Bonds comparison just got very real. ⚾ #Phillies

article.headline: Kyle Schwarber Hit His 20th Homer Four Days Faster Than Bonds Did in His Record Season

stories.title: Guardians 4-3 Tigers — Bazzana HR, Smith 15th Save, 4-Game Win Streak (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Travis Bazzana went yard in the 4th. Brayan Rocchio drove in the go-ahead run in the 7th. Cade Smith notched his 15th save.

Guardians 4, Tigers 3. Cleveland's 4th straight win. Detroit's 4th straight loss. #Guardians #Tigers

facebook.long_form:
🔵 FOUR IN A ROW FOR CLEVELAND 🔵

The Cleveland Guardians keep winning close games, and they keep doing it the same way: get a big hit when they need it, play solid defense, and let Cade Smith close it out.

Tuesday night's script: Travis Bazzana hit a two-run homer in the fourth inning off Keider Montero to give Cleveland a 3-2 lead. Brayan Rocchio drove in the go-ahead run in the seventh with a groundout that scored Steven Kwan. And then Cade Smith — one of the best closers in baseball — struck out two Tigers hitters in the ninth for his 15th save in 17 opportunities.

Cleveland 4, Detroit 3.

The Guardians have now won four consecutive games. The Tigers have now lost four consecutive games. The AL Central race takes shape one close game at a time.

Spencer Torkelson hit a two-run homer for Detroit, and the Tigers had their moments. But Cleveland found a way, as Cleveland often does.

[How far can this Guardians win streak go?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Travis Bazzana homered, Brayan Rocchio drove in the go-ahead run, and Cade Smith notched save No. 15. Cleveland's 4th straight win, Detroit's 4th straight loss. The AL Central race is very much alive. #Guardians 🔵

stories.title: Braves 32-15 — MLB's Best Record Approaching the Memorial Day Barometer (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Memorial Day is 5 days away. The traditional barometer for real MLB contenders.

The Atlanta Braves are 32-15 — best record in baseball.

They are making their case in the clearest way possible. #Braves

facebook.long_form:
🏆 32-15. BEST RECORD IN BASEBALL. 🏆

There's a reason baseball people talk about Memorial Day as the first real measuring stick of the season. Teams that are genuinely good reveal themselves by then. Teams that are pretending struggle to maintain the illusion. And teams with the best record in baseball at Memorial Day — well, they usually have something real to build on.

The Atlanta Braves are 32-15. They have the best record in Major League Baseball heading into the final week before Memorial Day.

Chris Sale has been everything the Braves needed after signing him as the veteran anchor of their rotation. Austin Riley is driving in runs at the rate of a middle-of-the-order masher. The lineup is deep. The pitching is holding up.

This is a Braves team that had a rough patch in May — the Marlins shut them out 12-0 on Monday. But Atlanta has answered: they bounced back to win 8-4 on Tuesday and kept their position at the top of the standings.

Thirty-two wins. Best record in baseball. Memorial Day is five days away.

The Braves are making their case in real time.

[Who do you think has the best rotation in baseball right now?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: The Atlanta Braves are 32-15 — MLB's best record — with Memorial Day five days away. The traditional barometer for true contenders, and Atlanta is passing the test. Chris Sale anchoring the rotation, Austin Riley driving in runs, and the Braves keep finding ways to win. #Braves 🏆

stories.title: Cam Schlittler's 1.35 ERA — A Start to a Season Unseen Since Walter Johnson (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Through his first 9 starts of the season, no MLB pitcher has combined Cam Schlittler's ERA (1.35), strikeouts (59), walks (9), and HRs allowed (1) since Walter Johnson did it in 1913.

113 years. That's the gap. #Yankees

facebook.long_form:
⚡ 113 YEARS. THAT'S THE GAP. ⚡

When baseball historians have to reach back to 1913 to find a comparable performance, it means something extraordinary is happening.

The last time a pitcher opened a season the way Cameron Schlittler has opened 2026 was Walter Johnson — the legendary "Big Train" — in the year that Woodrow Wilson was president and the Federal Reserve didn't yet exist.

Here is what Schlittler has done through his first nine starts of the 2026 season:

• ERA: 1.35
• Strikeouts: 59
• Walks: 9
• Home runs allowed: 1
• Record: 5-1

No pitcher in the century-plus since Walter Johnson has walked to the mound for his 9th start of a season with those same combined numbers — 50+ strikeouts, fewer than 10 walks, no more than 1 home run allowed, and a sub-1.50 ERA.

The New York Yankees knew they were developing something special. But "first since Walter Johnson" is a category that belongs to a very short list.

Schlittler is 25 years old. The season is 47 games old. And the comparisons have already cleared the century mark.

[Is Schlittler for real? Who's the best young pitcher in baseball right now?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Cam Schlittler: 1.35 ERA. 59 strikeouts. 9 walks. 1 HR allowed. Through 9 starts. The last time any pitcher opened a season with those combined numbers was Walter Johnson — in 1913. That's 113 years ago. The Yankees rookie might be something very special. ⚡ #Yankees

Good baseball incoming. #MLB

facebook.long_form:
📺 WEDNESDAY MLB: WHAT TO WATCH 📺

The midweek slate brings some compelling threads to follow:

NATIONALS-METS G3: James Wood just hit the first inside-the-park grand slam since 2022 to power Washington's rally Tuesday. Can the Nationals build on the momentum in the series finale?

PHILLIES IN ACTION: Kyle Schwarber has 20 home runs — the most in baseball — after reaching the milestone on the earliest date in MLB history. Every Phillies at-bat is now an HR watch.

GUARDIANS-TIGERS G3: Cleveland has won 4 straight; Detroit has lost 4 straight. Something changes today, or one team starts to separate themselves in the AL Central.

Check your local listings for game times. Baseball Wednesday is going to be a good one.
